qa_pairs.question: What is the policy number for this whole life insurance policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Policy No: GFP-WL-2024-8847

qa_pairs.question: What is the face amount of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Face Amount: $250,000

qa_pairs.question: What is the annual premium for this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Annual Premium: $3,240

qa_pairs.question: What is the maturity date of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Maturity Date: Age 100 (July 8, 2078)

qa_pairs.question: What is the insured's issue age?
qa_pairs.evidence: Issue Age: 45

qa_pairs.question: What is the insured's risk class?
qa_pairs.evidence: Risk Class: Standard Non-Tobacco

qa_pairs.question: What is the policy loan interest rate?
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.3 Loan interest rate: 5% per annum, compounded annually.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum loan amount available as a percentage of cash surrender value?
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.2 Maximum loan amount: 90% of current cash surrender value.

qa_pairs.question: What is the guaranteed cash value at the end of Year 10?
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.3 Guaranteed cash value at end of Year 10: $28,450

qa_pairs.question: What is the guaranteed cash value at the end of Year 20?
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.3 Guaranteed cash value at end of Year 20: $71,200

qa_pairs.question: How long is the free look period?
qa_pairs.evidence: 12. FREE LOOK PERIOD: You may cancel this policy within 30 days of receipt for a full refund of premiums paid.

qa_pairs.question: What is the interest rate charged on overdue premiums when reinstating a lapsed policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: 10.1 You may reinstate this policy within 3 years of lapse if: (b) You pay all overdue premiums with interest at 6% per annum

qa_pairs.question: Within how many days will the insurer pay death benefit claims after receiving complete documentation?
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.2 Payment will be made within 7 days of receiving complete claim documentation.

qa_pairs.question: What is the effective date of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Effective Date: February 1, 2024

qa_pairs.question: What company issued this whole life policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Guardian Family Protection Corp.

qa_pairs.question: Who is the primary beneficiary of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Primary: David Chen (Husband) - 100%

qa_pairs.question: Who is the contingent beneficiary of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Contingent: Chen Family Trust - 100%

qa_pairs.question: Who is the insured on this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Name: Margaret Rose Chen

qa_pairs.question: Which dividend option is currently selected on this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: [Current selection: Paid-Up Additions]

qa_pairs.question: What formula is used to calculate the death benefit payment?
qa_pairs.answer: Face Amount + Paid-Up Additions minus Policy Loans minus Loan Interest
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.1 Upon receipt of proof of death, we will pay the beneficiary: Face Amount + Paid-Up Additions - Policy Loans - Loan Interest

qa_pairs.question: What happens if the total indebtedness on a policy loan exceeds the cash value?
qa_pairs.answer: The policy will terminate unless sufficient payment is made within 31 days of notice.
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.5 If total indebtedness exceeds the cash value, the policy will terminate unless sufficient payment is made within 31 days of notice.

qa_pairs.question: What automatically applies if no non-forfeiture election is made within 60 days of lapse?
qa_pairs.answer: Extended Term Insurance will automatically apply.
qa_pairs.evidence: 9.4 If no election is made within 60 days of lapse, Extended Term Insurance will automatically apply.

qa_pairs.question: What is the insurer's liability if the insured dies by suicide within two years of the issue date?
qa_pairs.answer: The insurer will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.
qa_pairs.evidence: 11.1 SUICIDE: If the insured dies by suicide within two years from the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.

qa_pairs.question: Under what conditions may the insurer contest this policy?
qa_pairs.answer: The insurer may contest only within the first two years while the insured is living, and only for material misrepresentation in the application.
qa_pairs.evidence: 13. CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy only within the first two years while the insured is living, and only for material misrepresentation in the application.

qa_pairs.question: After how many years in force may the policy owner take a policy loan?
qa_pairs.answer: After the policy has been in force for one full year.
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.1 You may borrow against the cash value after the policy has been in force for one full year.

qa_pairs.question: What are the three conditions that must be met to reinstate a lapsed policy?
qa_pairs.answer: The policy owner must provide evidence of insurability satisfactory to the insurer, pay all overdue premiums with interest at 6% per annum, and repay or reinstate any policy loans with interest.
qa_pairs.evidence: 10.1 You may reinstate this policy within 3 years of lapse if: (a) You provide evidence of insurability satisfactory to us (b) You pay all overdue premiums with interest at 6% per annum (c) You repay or reinstate any policy loans with interest

qa_pairs.question: If the policy owner stops paying premiums after three years and elects Reduced Paid-Up Insurance, what happens?
qa_pairs.answer: Coverage continues for a reduced death benefit with no further premiums required; the amount is based on the cash value and the insured's attained age.
qa_pairs.evidence: 9.2 REDUCED PAID-UP INSURANCE: Continue coverage for a reduced death benefit with no further premiums required. Amount based on cash value and insured's attained age.

qa_pairs.question: If age was misstated in the application, how is the face amount adjusted?
qa_pairs.answer: The face amount is adjusted to what the premium would have purchased at the correct age.
qa_pairs.evidence: 11.2 MISSTATEMENT OF AGE: If the insured's age was misstated, we will adjust the face amount to what the premium would have purchased at the correct age.

qa_pairs.question: If the policy owner stops paying premiums after three years and does not make a non-forfeiture election within 60 days, and the insured then dies, what death benefit amount will be paid?
qa_pairs.answer: Extended Term Insurance automatically applies, continuing the full face amount for a limited period based on the cash value; the full face amount would be payable if death occurs within that limited period.
qa_pairs.evidence: 9.3 EXTENDED TERM INSURANCE: Continue the full face amount for a limited period based on cash value. No further premiums. / 9.4 If no election is made within 60 days of lapse, Extended Term Insurance will automatically apply.

qa_pairs.question: Is the actual cash value of this policy guaranteed to match the values shown in the policy values table?
qa_pairs.answer: The policy guarantees minimum cash values per the schedule, but actual cash value may be higher due to dividend credits.
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.1 This policy builds cash value according to the guaranteed schedule shown in the policy values table. / 5.4 Actual cash value may be higher due to dividend credits.

qa_pairs.question: Are dividends guaranteed to be declared each year?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Dividends are not guaranteed; they may be declared annually based on the company's mortality, investment, and expense experience.
qa_pairs.evidence: 6.1 This is a participating policy. Annual dividends may be declared based on our mortality, investment, and expense experience. / 6.2 Dividends are not guaranteed.
